example_prompt: Initiate a refund for a completed payment transaction.

transactionId.description: UUID of the original payment transaction to refund. Must be in 'succeeded' status.

response_schema.description: HTTP 202 — refund accepted and queued for processing. No body is returned. Monitor completion via webhook (type: refund, status: SUCCEEDED) or by calling verify_transaction with the original transactionId.

details.description: Bictorys error code. Examples: "E404-1: Cannot find resource", "E400-0: Bad transactionId is passed".

gotchas: This endpoint returns HTTP 202 (accepted), not 200. The refund is processed asynchronously — Bictorys fires a webhook event with type 'refund' and status 'SUCCEEDED' when the refund completes. Do not treat 202 as confirmation of completion.
The transactionId must refer to a succeeded payment transaction. Attempting to refund a pending, failed, or already-refunded transaction will return a 400 error.
Refunds may take time depending on the mobile money operator or card network. Timelines vary by provider.
Sandbox base URL is https://api.test.bictorys.com — the production URL is https://api.bictorys.com.
